focus: Sync latest demand-data-generation active artifact and make senior living demand available in assessment reports.
reasonStarted: The upstream demand source added a senior_living sector with Keyword Planner monthly counts for some search terms.

new: A senior living website should be classified as Senior Living demand when public page evidence contains senior living service terms.
Senior living demand records from the active upstream artifact should be evaluated in Customer Demand Fit.
Demand opportunities should continue to show estimated monthly searches when Keyword Planner count data is available.
Demand records with null monthly_searches should continue to render safely without inventing volume.
Senior living demand gaps should keep found evidence, missing signals, confidence, and pages checked traceable.
reused: Generated demand data remains owned by demand-data-generation and consumed as a release artifact.
Unsupported or unclear website industries skip demand scoring honestly.
Monthly search count helps order opportunities without replacing the existing priority-weighted demand score.

architecturalChanges: Added senior_living as a supported demand sector in the assessment app.
Preserved optional monthly search volume behavior added in Generation 41.

validation.build: passed with existing documented Turbopack tracing warning
validation.cloudflareBuild: passed with existing documented OpenNext tsconfig warning
validation.builtPackageSmoke: Senior Living assessed with 60 records evaluated and 34 records with monthly search counts.

knownRisks: The assessment app still consumes demand data by copied release artifact; automated upstream sync remains deferred.
Monthly search counts are available only for some senior living terms and are displayed only when present.
